results.rationale: At line 374 `deflated` receives the buffer allocated by zlib_deflate_raw. If the buffer deflates but compressed_size >= size (lines 377-380), method is reset to ZIP_METHOD_STORE while `deflated` still owns the allocation. The path then enters the STORE branch at line 426, and on a stream read error the function returns early at line 443 (`if (readlen) return readlen;`) without freeing `deflated`. This early return bypasses the normal end-of-function cleanup, leaking the deflate buffer. The path is feasible.
results.fix_suggestion: Free `deflated` before the early `return readlen;` at line 443 (e.g. `free(deflated); return readlen;`).

results.rationale: original_message is obtained from repo_logmsg_reencode at line 127, which returns either a newly reencoded buffer or the commit's cached buffer that must be released with repo_unuse_commit_buffer/logmsg_free. The out: cleanup block (154-156) frees original_extra_headers, commit_message, and original_author but never releases original_message, so on every path through the function the reencoded buffer leaks.
results.fix_suggestion: Add repo_unuse_commit_buffer(repo, commit_with_message, original_message); in the out: cleanup block before returning.

results.rationale: repo_logmsg_reencode at line 127 may return a newly allocated reencoded buffer that the caller owns and must release (via unuse_commit_buffer/logmsg_free). original_message is used at lines 128-131 but the out: cleanup (lines 154-156) only frees original_extra_headers, commit_message, and original_author. On every path original_message is leaked, and find_commit_header(original_message,...) at line 128 also lacks a NULL guard on the returned pointer.
results.fix_suggestion: At the out: label call repo_unuse_commit_buffer(repo, commit_with_message, original_message); to release the reencoded message buffer, and guard the line 128 dereference if original_message can be NULL.

results.rationale: ce is allocated at line 427 via make_empty_cache_entry. On the success path it is handed to add_index_entry (ownership transfer). But when add_index_entry fails (line 438), the function returns an error at lines 439-440 without freeing ce, leaking the allocation on that path. The leak's reality depends on whether the index uses a mem_pool for cache entries; if not pool-backed, this is a genuine leak.
results.fix_suggestion: On add_index_entry failure, call discard_cache_entry(ce) before returning the error.

results.rationale: make_empty_cache_entry allocates 'ce' at line 427. Ownership is transferred to the index only when add_index_entry succeeds. On the error return at lines 438-440 (add_index_entry returns non-zero), 'ce' is not added to the index and is never freed, leaking the allocation on that path. Note: if the index uses a mem_pool (split index), the entry is pool-owned and this becomes a false positive, which lowers confidence.
results.fix_suggestion: On the add_index_entry failure path, free the cache entry before returning (e.g., discard_cache_entry(ce)) unless it is pool-allocated.

results.rationale: new_base_hash (line 2210) and new_base_name (2211) are stored into ctx->commit_graph_hash_after/filenames_after (2215-2216), so ownership transfers to ctx. These arrays are freed only by the cleanup loop at 2733-2738. However, when ctx->split is taken, the function has early error returns (return -1 at 2231 when chainf is NULL, and at 2247 when rename fails) that bypass the 2733 cleanup loop. On those feasible paths the heap stored in ctx arrays is leaked. This matches the PartialLeak classification (freed on the normal path, leaked on the conditional error paths).
results.fix_suggestion: Replace the early `return -1` statements in the ctx->split block with a `goto cleanup` that jumps to the 2729-2738 free loop (and free chainf/lock), ensuring ctx arrays are released on all paths.

results.rationale: tagged_blobs is allocated at line 378 when info->blobs is set. On the normal exit paths it is either handed to strmap_put (ownership transferred, line 465) or freed (line 469), so the reported 389-False/461-False path itself frees the memory. However, the function has a feasible early-return leak: at line 403, when lookup_tag fails inside the loop, the function does `return -1` without freeing tagged_blobs (nor tags). results.rationale: iter is allocated by refs_ref_iterator_begin at line 3320. The only consumer that frees it is do_for_each_ref_iterator at line 3330, which runs solely when (!ret). If start_ref_iterator_after (3324) or ref_iterator_seek (3326) returns non-zero, do_for_each_ref_iterator is skipped and iter is never released before the function returns at 3348, leaking the iterator on the error path.
results.fix_suggestion: On the error path (when ret is non-zero after seek/start_after) call ref_iterator_free(iter) before returning, so the iterator is released even when do_for_each_ref_iterator is not invoked.

results.rationale: iter is allocated by refs_ref_iterator_begin at line 2771. On the path where start_ref_iterator_after sets ret != 0, the function returns at line 2777 without releasing iter. The normal path passes iter to do_for_each_ref_iterator, which consumes/frees it, but the early error return leaks the iterator.
results.fix_suggestion: On the ret != 0 error path, release the iterator before returning (e.g. call ref_iterator_free(iter) prior to 'return ret;').

results.rationale: iter is allocated at line 3320 via refs_ref_iterator_begin. It is only consumed (and freed) by do_for_each_ref_iterator at line 3330, which runs only when ret==0. If start_ref_iterator_after (3324) or ref_iterator_seek (3326) returns non-zero, the !ret branch is skipped and iter is never passed to do_for_each_ref_iterator, leaking the iterator. The seek/start_after functions do not free the iterator on failure, so the error path leaks. This is a feasible path; the reported null-deref aspect is less likely since refs_ref_iterator_begin typically returns a non-NULL (possibly empty) iterator.
results.fix_suggestion: Free the iterator on the seek/start_after error path, e.g. call ref_iterator_free(iter) (or ensure do_for_each_ref_iterator/abort runs) when ret is non-zero before returning.

results.rationale: filter is allocated at line 690. If reftable_buf_add fails at line 698 (err<0), control jumps to the 'out' label where only ti is freed (lines 709-711). filter is never released on this error path, leaking the allocation (and its embedded oid buffer). On the success path ownership transfers via iterator_from_filtering_ref_iterator, but the post-allocation error path leaks.
results.fix_suggestion: In the err<0 cleanup block, also release filter (and its buffer), e.g. reftable_buf_release(&filter->oid) and reftable_free(filter), when ownership was not transferred to the iterator.

results.rationale: filter is allocated at line 690 and only transferred to the output iterator on the success path (line 703). results.rationale: make_cache_entry() allocates a cache_entry at line 146. On the success path, add_index_entry() takes ownership and inserts ce into the index. But on the error path (add_index_entry returns non-zero at line 149), the function returns error() at line 150 without freeing ce, and add_index_entry does not take ownership on failure. The allocated entry leaks on that path.
results.fix_suggestion: On the add_index_entry failure path, free/discard ce before returning, e.g. call discard_cache_entry(ce) (or free(ce)) prior to the error() return at line 150.

results.rationale: fill_tree_descriptor at line 4070 allocates desc.buffer (freed only at the cleanup label, line 4094). The early return at line 4082 (when repo_parse_tree_indirect returns NULL) bypasses the cleanup label entirely, leaking desc.buffer as well as ref_name and unpack_tree_opts resources. All other error paths use 'goto cleanup', but this one returns directly.
results.fix_suggestion: Replace the direct 'return error(...)' at line 4082 with 'ret = error(...); goto cleanup;' so desc.buffer and other resources are released.

results.rationale: fill_tree_descriptor at line 4070 populates desc.buffer (allocated memory). On the path where unpack_trees succeeds but repo_parse_tree_indirect returns NULL (line 4081), the code does a direct `return error(...)` at line 4082 instead of `goto cleanup`. This bypasses `free((void *)desc.buffer)` at line 4094, leaking the tree buffer (and also skipping rollback_lock_file, strbuf_release, and clear_unpack_trees_porcelain). All other failure paths use goto cleanup, but this one returns early.
results.fix_suggestion: Replace the early `return error(...)` at line 4082 with `ret = error(...); goto cleanup;` so desc.buffer and other resources are released.
